response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and distributed-systems engineer, but his background is overwhelmingly web-centric (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with only a brief six-month C++ stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project. The role demands deep, career-long C++/STL expertise as its primary differentiator, combined with industrial automation protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), secure development under IEC 62443, and embedded/IoT systems experience — none of which appear in this resume. His skill set would be a strong fit for a senior web or platform engineering role, but it does not meet the core technical requirements here.
strengths: Demonstrated event-driven and distributed-systems thinking across multiple roles (GISCollective, Dunnhumby high-throughput micro-services)
Node.js and React experience aligns with the listed advantageous skills
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and cross-functional collaboration are well-evidenced
concerns: C++ experience is negligible — one six-month role and a thesis project over a 16-year career; C++ is listed nowhere in his skills section
Zero exposure to industrial automation, IoT protocols (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or embedded/on-premises platform work
No evidence of secure development lifecycle practices, IEC 62443, or any industrial cybersecurity context
